title: Midnight Garlic Haven
description: A peppery Malaysian dish featuring wild garlic leaves that satisfies midnight cravings, gluten-free and suitable for all diets.
instructions: Clean and chop the wild garlic leaves into small pieces.
In a wok, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and red chili peppers, sauté for about 2 minutes until fragrant but not browned.
Fold in chopped wild garlic leaves and continue to stir-fry for another minute. Add fish sauce (sambal), tamarind paste, and salt. Stir well until the flavors are evenly distributed.
Add water or chicken stock and bring it to a simmer. Cook until the liquid reduces slightly, about 5-7 minutes.
Once reduced, turn off heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving over steamed rice.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
